blocks.body: Present Continuous: Time Expressions — now, right now, at the moment, today, this week.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"The words now, right now, at the moment, today, this week, these days often point to Present Continuous.","rules":[{"label":"Now / Right now","color":"emerald","text":"These expressions often show an action at the exact moment of speaking.","example":"She is talking on the phone now."},{"label":"At the moment","color":"blue","text":"This expression also highlights an action happening right now.","example":"We are eating at the moment."},{"label":"Today / This week / These days","color":"amber","text":"These expressions often show an action in the current period, not only this second.","example":"I am working from home this week."}]}

{"title":"1. Useful time expressions","intro":"Start with the most common time markers.","items":[{"label":"Now","title":"now / right now","subtitle":"I am doing my homework now. / He is driving right now."},{"label":"Moment","title":"at the moment","subtitle":"They are waiting at the moment."},{"label":"Current period","title":"today / this week / this month","subtitle":"She is studying a lot this week."},{"label":"These days","title":"these days","subtitle":"We are taking the bus these days."}]}

{"title":"2. How these words work","sections":[{"label":"Moment of speaking","color":"emerald","description":"Use now, right now, and at the moment for an action happening right now.","examples":[{"en":"I am washing the dishes now.","ua":"I am washing the dishes now."},{"en":"She is sleeping at the moment.","ua":"She is sleeping at the moment."}]},{"label":"Current period","color":"blue","description":"Today, this week, this month, and these days often show a temporary action in the current period.","examples":[{"en":"He is staying with his cousin this week.","ua":"He is staying with his cousin this week."},{"en":"We are learning a new topic these days.","ua":"We are learning a new topic these days."}]},{"label":"Position in the sentence","color":"amber","description":"These time expressions often go at the end of the sentence, but they can also go at the beginning.","examples":[{"en":"She is working now. / Now she is working.","ua":"Both are possible."},{"en":"They are studying this week.","ua":"The time expression often goes at the end."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Typical combinations","intro":"See how time expressions combine with Present Continuous.","rows":[{"en":"I am reading now.","ua":"action at the moment of speaking","note":"now"},{"en":"She is talking right now.","ua":"action happening right now","note":"right now"},{"en":"We are working at the moment.","ua":"action at this moment","note":"at the moment"},{"en":"They are staying here this week.","ua":"temporary action in the current period","note":"this week"}],"warning":"Words like every day or on Mondays usually go with Present Simple, not Present Continuous."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using Present Simple with now for an action happening right now.","wrong":"❌ She works now.","right":"✅ She is working now."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Leaving out be before -ing with a time expression.","wrong":"❌ They waiting at the moment.","right":"✅ They are waiting at the moment."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using the base form after be.","wrong":"❌ I am study this week.","right":"✅ I am studying this week."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["now, right now, at the moment often show an action happening right now.","today, this week, this month, these days often show a temporary action in the current period.","A time expression often goes at the end of the sentence.","If the action is happening now, Present Continuous is often more natural than Present Simple."]}
